documentation.value: Reverts the transaction if the condition is false. Optionally provides an error message.
The boolean condition that must be true
Optional revert reason string
Checks for internal errors and invariants. Should only fail due to bugs. Consumes all remaining gas on failure.
The invariant condition that should always be true
Aborts execution and reverts state changes, providing an optional explanation string.
The revert reason string
Computes the Keccak-256 hash of the input data.
The bytes input to hash (use `abi.encodePacked(...)` for multiple values)
ABI-encodes the given arguments with padding to 32 bytes per value.
The values to ABI-encode
Performs non-standard packed encoding of the given values. Does not pad to 32 bytes.
The values to encode in packed format
ABI-decodes the given data according to the specified types.
The ABI-encoded bytes to decode
The tuple of types to decode into, e.g. `(uint256, address)`
Sends the given amount of Wei to the address. Reverts on failure. Forwards 2300 gas.
The amount of Wei to transfer
Sends the given amount of Wei to the address. Returns false on failure. Forwards 2300 gas.
The amount of Wei to send

signatures.label: address.call{value: uint256, gas: uint256}(data: bytes): (bool, bytes)

documentation.value: Low-level call to an address. Returns success boolean and return data.
The calldata to send
Declares a mapping type that maps keys of `keyType` to values of `valueType`.
The key type (must be a value type, bytes, string, or contract)
The value type (can be any type including another mapping)
The address of the account that sent the current call/transaction.
